# How to use opt to optimize the bitcode file generated by hipcc under ROCm-4.3.0

- **Issue #:** 1585
- **State:** closed
- **Created:** 2021-10-08T06:21:21Z
- **Updated:** 2022-02-08T10:05:31Z
- **URL:** https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/issues/1585

**Problem:**
I want to write custom pass to optimize a HIP program via the tool "opt" in llvm, but I found that opt cannot read the bitcode file generated by hipcc because of unrecognized entity. What should I do if I want to achieve my goal?
![02X)BUV387TX~ A36C {X)G](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31980988/136506402-a09c0e24-89c2-409c-b74d-58fd47380104.png)


**Reproduce Step:**
1. Write a code file like the one below.

```
#include<iostream>
#include<fstream>
#include<string>
void output_dimension_file(int M, int N, int K, std::string path)
{
    std::fstream fs(path,std::ios::app);
    if(!fs)
    {
        std::cout<<"Cant open the dimension file"<<std::endl;
        exit(1);
    }
    else
    {
        fs<<M<<" "<<N<<" "<<K<<std::endl;
        fs.close();
    }
}

void GemmEx(int a, int b, int c, int d, int e, int f)
{
    //do nothing so compile with -fno-inline
    return;
}

int main()
{
    int args[6] = {1,2,3,4,5,6};
    GemmEx(args[0],args[1],args[2],args[3],args[4],args[5]);
    return 0;
}
```

2. Use following command to get the bitcode file
```
hipcc -emit-llvm ~/workspace/test_space/llvm_test/mark_test.cpp -c -o mark_test_hip.bc
```

3. Use opt tool to optimize the bitcode
```
opt -load ~/hipcc_llvm_4.3.0/include/llvm/Transforms/build/Mark_Dimension_FuncID/libMDFID.so -mdfid -enable-new-pm=0 -debug-pass-manager < ~/workspace/test_space/llvm_test/mark_test_hip.bc -f
```


**Test Enviornment:**
1. GPU: MI100
2. ROCm: 4.3.0 
3. Compiler: ( /mnt/data/home/mzw/hipcc_llvm_4.3.0/ is copied directly from /opt/rocm-4.3.0/llvm/ )
```
mzw@user:~/workspace/test_space/llvm_test$ which hipcc
/opt/rocm-4.3.0/bin/hipcc
mzw@user:~/workspace/test_space/llvm_test$ which clang++
/mnt/data/home/mzw/hipcc_llvm_4.3.0/bin/clang++
mzw@user:~/workspace/test_space/llvm_test$ which opt
/mnt/data/home/mzw/hipcc_llvm_4.3.0/bin/opt
```
P.S.: I can compile normal HIP program via hipcc and run them on my GPU.

Any suggestions will be highly appreciated, thank you!
